hard_gate_results.reason: Pinned upstream baseline artifact is present and referenced by active parity artifacts.
Drop-in feature inventory artifacts are present in both machine-readable and markdown forms.
Gap ledger exists with owned entries and closure paths for known mismatches.

CLI parity gaps resolved through comprehensive test coverage. Extension flag pass-through implemented (tests/extension_flag_passthrough.rs), slash command differential parity verified (tests/dropin_slash_differential.rs), and edge case coverage expanded (tests/cli_edge_cases.rs with 40+ test functions).

JSON/RPC parity validated through comprehensive differential testing. RPC surface audit (bd-lnmtp.2.1), command semantics (bd-lnmtp.2.2), differential harness with 96.6% success rate (bd-lnmtp.2.3), and extension UI round-trip parity (bd-lnmtp.2.4) all completed.
Strict SDK drop-in claim officially retired per bd-lnmtp.3.1 decision. Rust SDK provides idiomatic companion API with functional equivalence.
Critical/high config gaps resolved (gap-config-defaults-compaction-retry). Remaining medium gaps (UI editor knobs, env classification) do not block certification.
Session JSONL schema and hostcall error taxonomy parity are covered by docs/dropin-error-crosswalk.json and tests/dropin_session_error_parity.rs.
Tool stdio, process lifecycle, file/search I/O, truncation metadata, and SSE chunk framing are covered by tests/dropin_tool_io_differential.rs and the G09 scenario fixture.
Differential-testing hard gate domain remains open and not fully closed with certifying evidence.
Quality-gates domain remains open; release gate audit currently reports failing evidence-contract status.
Parity documents are now conditioned on drop-in certification verdict instead of strict replacement claims.

evidence_index.description: Normative hard-gate contract and verdict artifact requirements
Owned parity gaps with severity and closure paths
Implementation matrix with strict-claim guardrail language
Recent evidence-contract artifact used by release gate checks
G09 executable tool I/O parity harness covering stdio, process lifecycle, file/search tools, truncation, and SSE framing
G05 JSON/RPC parity differential harness with 96.6% success rate (28/29 scenarios) covering command semantics, canonicalized event-stream comparison, and subprocess execution validation
G09 scenario manifest with more than 30 executable integration I/O parity cases
G09 integration I/O parity evidence summary and canonicalization rules
G06-T1 decision artifact: retire strict SDK drop-in claim (bd-lnmtp.3.1)
G04 extension flag pass-through conformance tests covering CLI flag parsing and routing
G04 comprehensive CLI argument parsing edge cases with 40+ test functions covering quoting, environment expansion, @file references, and positional precedence
G04 slash command differential parity harness verifying command surface equivalence with pi-mono
